chunk_text: # Task T1 - Plan for MCP-specs

title: Instructions
chunk_text:
## Requirements
1. understand the `MCP-specs.md`
2. interview me for any info you need
3. search the web to check issues, gaps, and opportunities to simplify or improve the specs.
4. Split the specs into independent, implementable, verifiable tasks. These tasks incrementally build towards the full implementation.
5. save each task to `.lissom/tasks/<task_id>/Specs.md`, starting from `IMPL-1`

title: MCP 0
chunk_text:
# Task MCP-0 - Design Decision Record MCP Server

> A read-only MCP server that lets agents find Design Decision Records explaining why code looks the way it does. Indexing is a separate CLI concern — the server only serves.

---

title: MCP 0
chunk_text:
## Overview

`ddr-mcp` is a single binary with two subcommands:

- `ddr-mcp index` — build or update the local vector index from source documents (any text files)
- `ddr-mcp serve` — start the MCP server and expose `search_ddr` over HTTP

The agent only ever sees `search_ddr`. Indexing is a developer or CI operation, completely outside the MCP surface.

---

title: MCP 0
chunk_text:
### `ddr-mcp index`

Incrementally updates the index by default. Compares each source file's SHA-256 hash against what is stored in the index:

- New files → chunk, embed, add to index
- Changed files → re-chunk, re-embed, replace in index
- Deleted files → remove from index
- Unchanged files → skip

```text
ddr-mcp index [flags] File

Flags:
  --config <path>    Path to config file (default: ./config.toml)
  --rebuild          Wipe the existing index and re-embed everything from scratch
  --git              Index Git history from current branch. File must be a directory.
File                 Path to a file or dir to index.
```

`--rebuild` wipe out the existing index and rebuild. Prompt the user for confirmation if the index already exists:
```text
Warning: this will delete the existing index at '<persist_path>' and rebuild it from scratch.
Are you sure? (y/N)
```
When `--rebuild` is absent, the source is refreshed or added to the existing index.

title: MCP 0
chunk_text:
### `ddr-mcp serve`

Starts the MCP server over HTTP.

```
ddr-mcp serve [flags]

Flags:
  --config <path>    Path to config file (default: ./config.toml under user working directory)
```

**Startup checks (in order):**

1. Load and validate `config.toml`. Exit with error if invalid.
2. Check that an index exists at `persist_path`. If not:
   ```
   Error: no index found at '<persist_path>'.
   Run 'ddr-mcp index' to build it.
   ```
3. Read the index header and validate it against the current config. If there is a mismatch (model name, chunk size, or schema version), e.g.:
   ```
   Error: index was built with embedding_model "<model_in_index>",
          but config specifies "<model_in_config>".
   Run 'ddr-mcp index --rebuild' to rebuild the index.
   ```
4. Load the index into memory.
5. Start the HTTP server and respond to the MCP `initialize` handshake.

---

title: MCP 0
chunk_text:
## Configuration

Single shared `config.toml` used by both subcommands. This config file is placed in a project dir. While `ddr-mcp` is a binary installed globally.

```toml
[index]
embedding_model = "BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5"   # downloaded from HuggingFace on first run, cached locally in user home directory for reuse.
persist_path    = "./.ddr-index"              # directory for index files and model cache
chunk_size      = 512                         # tokens per chunk
chunk_overlap   = 64

[server]
log_level = "warn"    # debug | info | warn | error
```

---

title: MCP 0
chunk_text:
## Embedding Model

The server is fully self-contained — no external API, no API key, no network dependency after first run.

**Default:** `BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5` via [FastEmbed](https://github.com/qdrant/fastembed) (ONNX Runtime, quantized weights, CPU-optimised).

| Property | Value |
|---|---|
| Runtime | ONNX Runtime — no PyTorch, no CUDA |
| Model size | ~130 MB (downloaded once, cached at user directory `~/.cache/ddr-mcp/models/`) |
| Embedding dims | 384 |
| CPU speed | Fast — suitable for laptop indexing |
| First run | Downloads model weights automatically |
| Subsequent runs | Loads from local cache, no network needed |

**Swapping models:** Change `embedding_model` in config, then run `ddr-mcp index --rebuild`. The `serve` startup check refuses to load an index built with a different model, preventing silent vector space mismatches.

Recommended alternatives if retrieval quality needs improving:

| Model | Size | Dims | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `BAAI/bge-base-en-v1.5` | ~430 MB | 768 | Better quality, slower indexing |
| `nomic-ai/nomic-embed-text-v1.5` | ~270 MB | 768 | Strong on longer documents |
| `google/gemma-embedding-3-300m` | ~200 MB | 768 | Best-in-class quality under 500M params |

---

title: MCP 0
chunk_text:
## Index Format

The index is stored as a directory at `persist_path`:

```
.ddr-index/
  header.json       # metadata, validated on serve startup
  vectors.bin       # packed float32 vectors
  metadata.json     # per-chunk metadata (source path, hash, document fields)
```

`header.json` schema:

```json
{
  "schema_version": 1,
  "embedding_model": "BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5",
  "embedding_dims": 384,
  "chunk_size": 512,
  "chunk_overlap": 64,
  "built_at": "2025-03-14T10:00:00Z",
  "doc_count": 42,
  "chunk_count": 187
}
```

`ddr-mcp serve` validates `schema_version`, `embedding_model`, `chunk_size`, and `chunk_overlap` against the active config. Any mismatch → exit with error and instruct the user to run `ddr-mcp index --rebuild`.

---

title: MCP 0
chunk_text:
### Transport

| Property | Value |
|---|---|
| Protocol | MCP over http (JSON-RPC 2.0) |
| Framing | Newline-delimited JSON |
| Logs | stdio |

title: MCP 0
MCP 0
chunk_text:
### Tools

The server exposes exactly one tool.

---

title: MCP 0
chunk_text:
## Tool: `search_ddr`

Find design decision records explaining why code looks the way it does. Call this when you encounter a pattern, constraint, or implementation choice that seems non-obvious.

title: MCP 0
MCP 0
chunk_text:
### Response

An array of result objects, ordered by relevance score descending.

```json
[
  {
    "title": "012 cache strategy",
    "source_path": "docs/ddr/012-cache-strategy.md",
    "matched_content": "All cache writes are synchronous and blocking. We had a staging bug where a user updated their profile but saw stale data for up to 30s...",
    "score": 0.91
  }
]
```

title: MCP 0
chunk_text:
### Result fields

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `title` | string | yes | Display title derived from the source filename |
| `source_path` | string | yes | Path of the source document relative to repo root |
| `matched_content` | string | yes | The chunk text that matched the query |
| `score` | float | yes | Relevance score in [0, 1] |

---

title: MCP 0
chunk_text:
## Search Behaviour

1. Embed `query` using the local ONNX model (same model as the index).
2. Brute-force cosine similarity against all chunk vectors.
3. Deduplicate — if multiple chunks from the same source document are in the candidate set, keep only the highest-scoring chunk.
4. Return top `limit` results, sorted by final score descending.

---

title: MCP 0
chunk_text:
## Document Format

Source documents are **any text files**. The content is not parsed or interpreted — it is treated as opaque text, chunked, and embedded for semantic search.

A display title is derived from the filename (extension stripped, hyphens/underscores replaced with spaces).

title: MCP 0
MCP 0
MCP 0
chunk_text:
## CI Usage (Out of scope)

Because indexing is a separate command, it fits naturally into CI:

```yaml
# .github/workflows/ddr-index.yml
- run: ddr-mcp index
- uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
  with:
    name: ddr-index
    path: .ddr-index/
```

Developers download the artifact and never wait for local indexing. `ddr-mcp index --rebuild` can be scheduled weekly or triggered manually when the embedding model changes.

---

title: MCP 0
chunk_text:
## What the Server Does Not Do

- No indexing — that is `ddr-mcp index`'s job.
- No write tools — agents cannot create or update DDRs.
- No file watching or live reload.
- No authentication — trust is delegated to the process boundary.
- No streaming — the full result array is returned in one response.
- No cross-session state — every `search_ddr` call is fully stateless.
section_heading: What the Server Does Not Do
